name: Southwest® Rapid Rewards® Priority Credit Card
effective_cost: Highest fee in the family — needs frequent flying or A-List pursuit to clear

verdict.text: Only earns back its fee for frequent flyers chasing A-List

earn_note: The only uncapped bonus category in the family — Plus and Premier both cap their second category at $5k–$8k/yr — plus the fastest Southwest multiplier at 4×. Still a 1× base, though; a flat-rate card like Venture X beats it on uncategorized spend.

points.note: Same closed-loop Rapid Rewards currency and dynamic pricing (since March 2025) as the rest of the family — Priority just earns it fastest, especially on Southwest-direct spend.

transfer_partners.highlight: Rapid Rewards points only redeem for Southwest travel (or a short list of gift-card/merchandise partners at poor value) — there's no way to move them to another airline or hotel program.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: Closed-loop currency, unchanged in structure across all three Southwest personal cards. Chase Ultimate Rewards can convert into this account, but nothing moves out of it.

credits.name: Free Checked Bag
credits.subtitle: 1st bag · you + up to 8 on reservation
credits.description: Southwest ended free checked bags for everyone on May 28, 2025 ($35 first bag / $45 second bag on most fares). Rapid Rewards cardmembers keep their first checked bag free — for the cardholder and up to 8 companions on the same reservation.
tips: Only the first bag is free on this card, same as Plus and Premier — even Priority doesn't unlock a free second bag; that requires A-List Preferred status or a Choice Extra fare.
It covers everyone on the reservation, not just the cardholder — a family checking one bag each on a round trip can clear a meaningful chunk of the $229 fee in a single trip.

credits.name: Instacart+ Credit
credits.subtitle: $10/mo · through 2027 promo
credits.description: Up to $10/month in Instacart credit on one qualifying $10+ order, available with an active Instacart+ membership (3 months complimentary) — added in the July 2025 refresh, running through the Dec 31, 2027 promo window.
tips: Requires enrolling in Instacart+ (free for the first 3 months, a paid membership after) — only worth carrying if you'd keep the subscription anyway.
The monthly credit doesn't roll over — a month skipped is a month lost.

insurance.detail: Secondary (domestic) / primary outside the US · $60k · 31 days
Not offered on any Southwest personal card

protection_note: Identical Visa Signature protections to Plus and Premier — the $229 fee buys faster earning and better boarding/seating perks, not stronger insurance. Still no trip cancellation/interruption coverage at any tier, and rental car coverage is secondary domestically.
rental_note: Secondary for domestic rentals (pays after your own auto policy), primary only outside the US, capped at $60,000 and 31 consecutive days. Decline the counter's CDW and pay with the card, but don't rely on it as your only coverage for a US rental.

status_perks.name: Companion Pass progress
status_perks.note: A 10,000-point boost toward the 135,000 Companion Pass qualifying points needed each year — identical across all three Southwest cards, so it's not a reason to pick this tier over Plus or Premier.

status_perks.name: A-List tier points
status_perks.note: 2,500 Tier Qualifying Points per $5,000 spent (doubled from 1,500 in the July 2025 refresh) — the fastest card-spend path to the 35,000-TQP A-List bar of any Southwest personal card.

status_perks.name: Boarding position
status_perks.note: Group 5 boarding — the same slot as Plus and Premier — but paired with Preferred seat selection at booking (not just within 48 hours) and unlimited Extra Legroom upgrades within 48 hours of departure.

services.name: Seat selection & upgrades
services.detail: Preferred seat selection at booking (earlier than Plus/Premier's 48-hour window), plus unlimited complimentary upgrades to Extra Legroom seats within 48 hours of departure, when available — for you + up to 8 companions.

services.name: Anniversary bonus
services.detail: 7,500 Rapid Rewards points automatically deposited every account anniversary — the largest in the family.

services.name: Inflight purchase credit
services.detail: 25% back as a statement credit on Southwest inflight food, drinks and Wi-Fi purchases.

services.name: Partner perks (through 2027)
services.detail: 3-month complimentary Instacart+ trial (see the Instacart credit above), 12-month complimentary DashPass (activate by Dec 31, 2027), and 5× Rapid Rewards points on Lyft rides through Dec 2027 — the richest multiplier in the family.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: Zero FX fees on international spend.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards

benefits.text: Earns Rapid Rewards on their spend (into your account)
Own card number, contributes to the account's Companion Pass and A-List progress
Free-bag and boarding perks come from being booked on the same reservation, not from authorized-user status

additional_cards.note: Authorized users are free on all three Southwest personal cards. The free checked bag and Group 5 boarding apply to whoever is booked on the same reservation as the cardholder (up to 8 people) — you don't need to add someone as an authorized user for them to get it, just book them on your itinerary.

timeline.text: Southwest ended free checked bags for all passengers ($35 first bag / $45 second bag on most fares) — this card's free first bag went from a redundant perk to a real one overnight.
Fee raised $149 → $229; Southwest earn rate rose 3× → 4×, A-List tier points doubled to 2,500 per $5,000, and Extra Legroom upgrades plus earlier Preferred seat selection were added.
The $75 annual Southwest travel credit and the 4 annual Upgraded Boarding certificates from the old card structure were discontinued, along with 2× points on internet/cable/phone.
